characterViolations.violation:
Character may have used English in dialogue: "~She takes the jacket off the display and holds it up gently.~

"È in cashmere misto seta — leggeris"

Character may have used English in dialogue: "~She leads you toward the fitting room at the back of the store.~

"Certo! Il camerino è qui, prego."

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Soft music drifts through the boutique as you step inside.~

"Buongiorno! Benvenuti."

[ENGLISH: Good afternoon! Welcome.]

[HINT: Sto solo guardando.]
transcript.spoken:
~Soft music drifts through the boutique as you step inside.~

"Buongiorno! Benvenuti."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "Mi può aiutare?" 😄
transcript.spoken: "Mi può aiutare?" 😄

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina smiles warmly and steps forward.~

"Certo, con piacere! Cosa sta cercando oggi?"

[ENGLISH: Of course, with pleasure! What are you looking for today?]

[HINT: Try saying what you want — for example, "una giacca" (a jacket) or "un regalo" (a gift).]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina smiles warmly and steps forward.~

"Certo, con piacere! Cosa sta cercando oggi?"
transcript.hint: Try saying what you want — for example, "una giacca" (a jacket) or "un regalo" (a gift).

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "Sto solo guardando!" 😊
transcript.spoken: "Sto solo guardando!" 😊

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina gestures gracefully toward a display near the window.~

"Certo, si accomodi! Sa, abbiamo appena ricevuto questa giacca — è davvero speciale."

[ENGLISH: Of course, make yourself at home! You know, we just received this jacket — it's really special.]

[HINT: è bellissimo]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina gestures gracefully toward a display near the window.~

"Certo, si accomodi! Sa, abbiamo appena ricevuto questa giacca — è davvero speciale."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "È bellissimo!" 😍
transcript.spoken: "È bellissimo!" 😍

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She takes the jacket off the display and holds it up gently.~

"È in cashmere misto seta — leggerissima, ma caldissima. Artigianato italiano al cento per cento."

[ENGLISH: It's cashmere and silk blend — incredibly light, but very warm. One hundred percent Italian craftsmanship.]

[HINT: che taglia è]
transcript.spoken:
~She takes the jacket off the display and holds it up gently.~

"È in cashmere misto seta — leggerissima, ma caldissima. Artigianato italiano al cento per cento."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "Posso provarlo?" 😊
transcript.spoken: "Posso provarlo?" 😊

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She checks the tag with a practiced glance.~

"Che taglia porta, di solito?"

[ENGLISH: What size do you usually wear?]

[HINT: Try saying a size — for example, "taglia media" (medium) or "large."]
transcript.spoken:
~She checks the tag with a practiced glance.~

"Che taglia porta, di solito?"
transcript.hint: Try saying a size — for example, "taglia media" (medium) or "large."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "Che taglia è?" 😄
transcript.spoken: "Che taglia è?" 😄

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She turns the tag toward you.~

"È una taglia media. Ce l'abbiamo anche in large, se preferisce."

[ENGLISH: It's a medium. We also have it in large, if you prefer.]

[HINT: ce l'ha in nero]
transcript.spoken:
~She turns the tag toward you.~

"È una taglia media. Ce l'abbiamo anche in large, se preferisce."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "Posso provarlo?" 😊
transcript.spoken: "Posso provarlo?" 😊

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She leads you toward the fitting room at the back of the store.~

"Certo! Il camerino è qui, prego."

[ENGLISH: Of course! The fitting room is right here, please go ahead.]

[HINT: mi sta bene]
transcript.spoken:
~She leads you toward the fitting room at the back of the store.~

"Certo! Il camerino è qui, prego."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "Il camerino!" 😄 *points and nods enthusiastically*
transcript.spoken: "Il camerino!" 😄 *points and nods enthusiastically*

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She smiles and holds the curtain open for you.~

"Prego, si accomodi!"

[ENGLISH: Please, go right in!]

[HINT: mi sta bene]
transcript.spoken:
~She smiles and holds the curtain open for you.~

"Prego, si accomodi!"

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*steps inside and comes back out a moment later, beaming*

"Mi sta bene! È bellissimo!" 😄
transcript.spoken:
*steps inside and comes back out a moment later, beaming*

"Mi sta bene! È bellissimo!" 😄

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina steps back and looks at you with genuine appreciation.~

"Le sta benissimo — il colore è perfetto per lei."

[ENGLISH: It suits you wonderfully — the color is perfect for you.]

[HINT: lo prendo]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina steps back and looks at you with genuine appreciation.~

"Le sta benissimo — il colore è perfetto per lei."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*grins and looks in the mirror*

"Mi sta bene!" 😄
transcript.spoken:
*grins and looks in the mirror*

"Mi sta bene!" 😄

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She nods with quiet conviction.~

"Allora, lo prende?"

[ENGLISH: So, will you take it?]

[HINT: lo prendo]
transcript.spoken:
~She nods with quiet conviction.~

"Allora, lo prende?"

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*glances at the tag and winces*

"Troppo caro... Ci penso." 😬
transcript.spoken:
*glances at the tag and winces*

"Troppo caro... Ci penso." 😬

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She takes the jacket gracefully and nods with a warm smile.~

"Capisco perfettamente. Siamo aperti fino alle sette, se cambia idea."

[ENGLISH: I understand completely. We're open until seven, if you change your mind.]

[HINT: Try saying where you're going next — "Andiamo al Duomo" or "Andiamo a un caffè."]
transcript.spoken:
~She takes the jacket gracefully and nods with a warm smile.~

"Capisco perfettamente. Siamo aperti fino alle sette, se cambia idea."
transcript.hint: Try saying where you're going next — "Andiamo al Duomo" or "Andiamo a un caffè."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*nods and smiles warmly, backing toward the door*

"Ci penso! È bellissimo!" 😊
transcript.spoken:
*nods and smiles warmly, backing toward the door*

"Ci penso! È bellissimo!" 😊

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She walks you graciously toward the door.~

"Grazie mille! Dove va adesso?"

[ENGLISH: Thank you so much! Where are you going now?]

[HINT: Try "Andiamo al Duomo" or "Andiamo a un caffè" — where are you headed?]
transcript.spoken:
~She walks you graciously toward the door.~

"Grazie mille! Dove va adesso?"
transcript.hint: Try "Andiamo al Duomo" or "Andiamo a un caffè" — where are you headed?

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw: "Il Duomo!" 😄
transcript.spoken: "Il Duomo!" 😄

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
"Il Duomo! Dall'eleganza della moda all'eleganza gotica."

[ENGLISH: The Duomo! From the elegance of fashion to Gothic elegance.]

[DEBRIEF]
{
  "learned": ["sto solo guardando — I'm just looking", "mi può aiutare — can you help me", "è bellissimo — it's beautiful", "posso provarlo — can I try it on", "il camerino — the fitting room", "mi sta bene — it fits/suits me", "troppo caro — too expensive", "ci penso — I'll think about it", "lo prendo — I'll take it"],
  "retry": ["che taglia è — what size is it", "ce l'ha in nero — do you have it in black"],
  "valentina_says": "Chad, you were warm and enthusiastic from the moment you walked in — your Italian felt genuinely natural. The jacket truly did suit you, and I think you knew it too. Next time, try 'ce l'ha in nero' — sometimes the same piece in a different color changes everything.",
  "transitionTo": "duomo"
}
[/DEBRIEF]
transcript.spoken: "Il Duomo! Dall'eleganza della moda all'eleganza gotica."

learned: sto solo guardando — I'm just looking
mi può aiutare — can you help me
posso provarlo — can I try it on
mi sta bene — it fits/suits me
ci penso — I'll think about it
retry: che taglia è — what size is it
ce l'ha in nero — do you have it in black
debrief.valentina_says: Chad, you were warm and enthusiastic from the moment you walked in — your Italian felt genuinely natural. The jacket truly did suit you, and I think you knew it too. Next time, try 'ce l'ha in nero' — sometimes the same piece in a different color changes everything.

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Soft music, warm lighting. Valentina looks up from behind a display and smiles.~

"Buongiorno! Benvenuti."

[ENGLISH: Good afternoon! Welcome.]

[HINT: Sto solo guardando.]
transcript.spoken:
~Soft music, warm lighting. Valentina looks up from behind a display and smiles.~

"Buongiorno! Benvenuti."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
"Buongiorno! Grazie!" 

*looks around nervously, picks up a jacket*

"Sto solo... guardando. Sì!"
transcript.spoken:
"Buongiorno! Grazie!" 

*looks around nervously, picks up a jacket*

"Sto solo... guardando. Sì!"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina smiles warmly and takes a small step back.~

"Certo, si accomodi."

~She gestures toward a display near the window.~

"Quella sciarpa lì — è in cashmere puro, appena arrivata."

[ENGLISH: Of course, make yourself at home. That scarf there — it's in pure cashmere, just arrived.]

[HINT: È bellissima! — or — mi può aiutare]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina smiles warmly and takes a small step back.~

"Certo, si accomodi."

~She gestures toward a display near the window.~

"Quella sciarpa lì — è in cashmere puro, appena arrivata."
transcript.hint: È bellissima! — or — mi può aiutare

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*eyes light up at the scarf*

"Ohhh, è bellissimo! Cashmere... molto buono!"

*picks it up and touches it*

"Che... che taglia è? No no, scusa — " *laughs* "È per mia moglie. She's a... una donna... normale size?"
transcript.spoken:
*eyes light up at the scarf*

"Ohhh, è bellissimo! Cashmere... molto buono!"

*picks it up and touches it*

"Che... che taglia è? No no, scusa — " *laughs* "È per mia moglie. She's a... una donna... normale size?"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~She takes the scarf and holds it up, letting it catch the light.~

"È una taglia unica — va bene per tutti."

"Ce l'abbiamo in rosso, in blu, e in questo grigio elegante."

[ENGLISH: It's one size — it suits everyone. We have it in red, in blue, and in this elegant grey.]

[HINT: Ce l'ha in nero? — or — È bellissima!]
transcript.spoken:
~She takes the scarf and holds it up, letting it catch the light.~

"È una taglia unica — va bene per tutti."

"Ce l'abbiamo in rosso, in blu, e in questo grigio elegante."
transcript.hint: Ce l'ha in nero? — or — È bellissima!

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*nods enthusiastically*

"Ohhh, taglia unica, perfecto — " *catches himself* "...perfetto!"

*points to the red one*

"Mia moglie... lei ama il rosso. Rosso! Sì!"

*rubs fingers together* "Quanto costa?"
transcript.spoken:
*nods enthusiastically*

"Ohhh, taglia unica, perfecto — " *catches himself* "...perfetto!"

*points to the red one*

"Mia moglie... lei ama il rosso. Rosso! Sì!"

*rubs fingers together* "Quanto costa?"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina drapes the red scarf over her arm, showing the rich color.~

"Costa duecentocinquanta euro — è cashmere scozzese, fatto a mano."

[ENGLISH: It costs two hundred and fifty euros — it's Scottish cashmere, handmade.]

[HINT: Posso provarlo?]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina drapes the red scarf over her arm, showing the rich color.~

"Costa duecentocinquanta euro — è cashmere scozzese, fatto a mano."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*winces slightly, does the mental math*

"Duecentocinquanta... two hundred fifty..."

*exhales slowly*

"Mmmmm... troppo caro!"

*pauses, looks at the scarf again longingly*

"Forse... ci penso. Maybe — avete qualcosa... più piccolo? Più... economico?"
transcript.spoken:
*winces slightly, does the mental math*

"Duecentocinquanta... two hundred fifty..."

*exhales slowly*

"Mmmmm... troppo caro!"

*pauses, looks at the scarf again longingly*

"Forse... ci penso. Maybe — avete qualcosa... più piccolo? Più... economico?"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina nods graciously and moves toward a nearby display.~

"Certo. Abbiamo anche questi guanti in lana merino — ottima qualità, molto eleganti."

[ENGLISH: Of course. We also have these merino wool gloves — excellent quality, very elegant.]

[HINT: È bellissima! — or — Posso provarli?]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina nods graciously and moves toward a nearby display.~

"Certo. Abbiamo anche questi guanti in lana merino — ottima qualità, molto eleganti."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*picks up the gloves and examines them*

"Ohhh, i guanti! Molto belli!"

*tries to gesture size with hands*

"Mia moglie ha mani... piccole? Small hands?"

*looks up hopefully*

"Posso... posso provarlo? No no —" *laughs at himself* "Lei può provarlo? Il... fitting room? Il camerino?"
transcript.spoken:
*picks up the gloves and examines them*

"Ohhh, i guanti! Molto belli!"

*tries to gesture size with hands*

"Mia moglie ha mani... piccole? Small hands?"

*looks up hopefully*

"Posso... posso provarlo? No no —" *laughs at himself* "Lei può provarlo? Il... fitting room? Il camerino?"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina smiles and leads him toward the back of the store.~

"Certo! Il camerino è qui."

"Sua moglie può provare i guanti — la aspettiamo."

[ENGLISH: Of course! The fitting room is right here. His wife can try on the gloves — we'll wait for her.]

[HINT: Mi sta bene! — or — Mi sta un po' stretto.]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina smiles and leads him toward the back of the store.~

"Certo! Il camerino è qui."

"Sua moglie può provare i guanti — la aspettiamo."
transcript.hint: Mi sta bene! — or — Mi sta un po' stretto.

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*waves hands and laughs*

"No no no — mia moglie non è qui! She's at... ella è al... caffè? Vicino?"

*holds up the gloves*

"Io compro per lei — it's a surprise! Sorpresa!"

*grins hopefully*

"Che taglia è... per i guanti?"
transcript.spoken:
*waves hands and laughs*

"No no no — mia moglie non è qui! She's at... ella è al... caffè? Vicino?"

*holds up the gloves*

"Io compro per lei — it's a surprise! Sorpresa!"

*grins hopefully*

"Che taglia è... per i guanti?"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina smiles warmly and checks the tag on the gloves.~

"Ah, una sorpresa romantica! Questi sono taglia piccola — perfetti per mani delicate."

[ENGLISH: Ah, a romantic surprise! These are size small — perfect for delicate hands.]

[HINT: Ce l'ha in nero? — or — Lo prendo!]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina smiles warmly and checks the tag on the gloves.~

"Ah, una sorpresa romantica! Questi sono taglia piccola — perfetti per mani delicate."
transcript.hint: Ce l'ha in nero? — or — Lo prendo!

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*claps hands together excitedly*

"Taglia piccola — perfetto! Mia moglie ha mani... molto delicate, sì!"

*nods confidently, then hesitates*

"E quanto costa? I guanti?"
transcript.spoken:
*claps hands together excitedly*

"Taglia piccola — perfetto! Mia moglie ha mani... molto delicate, sì!"

*nods confidently, then hesitates*

"E quanto costa? I guanti?"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina turns over the small tag gently.~

"I guanti costano novantacinque euro."

[ENGLISH: The gloves cost ninety-five euros.]

[HINT: Lo prendo! — or — Ci penso.]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina turns over the small tag gently.~

"I guanti costano novantacinque euro."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*tilts head, calculating*

"Novantacinque... ninety-five..."

*nods slowly, patting wallet pocket*

"Okay... okay! Mi sta bene! No no —" *laughs* "È bene! Good price!"

*picks up the red scarf again and looks at it one more time with longing*

"...Solo i guanti. Lo prendo! I guanti!"
transcript.spoken:
*tilts head, calculating*

"Novantacinque... ninety-five..."

*nods slowly, patting wallet pocket*

"Okay... okay! Mi sta bene! No no —" *laughs* "È bene! Good price!"

*picks up the red scarf again and looks at it one more time with longing*

"...Solo i guanti. Lo prendo! I guanti!"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina smiles warmly and walks him toward the counter.~

"Perfetto! Sua moglie sarà felice — una bella sorpresa."

[ENGLISH: Perfect! His wife will be happy — a lovely surprise.]

[HINT: Grazie mille!]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina smiles warmly and walks him toward the counter.~

"Perfetto! Sua moglie sarà felice — una bella sorpresa."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*beams proudly*

"Sì sì! Lei sarà... very happy! Molto felice!"

*fumbles with wallet*

"Posso pagare con... carta? Credit card?"

*mimes swiping a card*
transcript.spoken:
*beams proudly*

"Sì sì! Lei sarà... very happy! Molto felice!"

*fumbles with wallet*

"Posso pagare con... carta? Credit card?"

*mimes swiping a card*

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina gestures to the card reader on the counter.~

"Sì, certo — accettiamo tutte le carte."

[ENGLISH: Yes, of course — we accept all cards.]

[HINT: Grazie mille! — or — Arrivederci!]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina gestures to the card reader on the counter.~

"Sì, certo — accettiamo tutte le carte."

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*taps card on the reader*

"Grazie! Molto grazie — " *catches himself* "MILLE grazie! Sì!"

*watches her wrap the gloves*

"Bellissimo! Mia moglie... she's gonna love it. Lei... amerà molto!"

*grins ear to ear*
transcript.spoken:
*taps card on the reader*

"Grazie! Molto grazie — " *catches himself* "MILLE grazie! Sì!"

*watches her wrap the gloves*

"Bellissimo! Mia moglie... she's gonna love it. Lei... amerà molto!"

*grins ear to ear*

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina places the gloves in an elegant bag tied with ribbon.~

"Grazie a lei — e complimenti per la scelta!"

"Allora, dove va adesso?"

[ENGLISH: Thank you — and congratulations on the choice! So, where are you off to now?]

[HINT: Vado al Duomo! — or — Vado al caffè.]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina places the gloves in an elegant bag tied with ribbon.~

"Grazie a lei — e complimenti per la scelta!"

"Allora, dove va adesso?"
transcript.hint: Vado al Duomo! — or — Vado al caffè.

transcript.speaker: Chad
transcript.raw:
*takes the bag carefully*

"Ohhh, grazie, grazie!"

*thinks for a moment*

"Adesso... vado al... Duomo? Sì! Il Duomo di Milano!"

*laughs and holds up the bag*

"E dopo — caffè con mia moglie. La sorpresa!"

*waves happily toward the door*

"Arrivederci, Valentina!"
transcript.spoken:
*takes the bag carefully*

"Ohhh, grazie, grazie!"

*thinks for a moment*

"Adesso... vado al... Duomo? Sì! Il Duomo di Milano!"

*laughs and holds up the bag*

"E dopo — caffè con mia moglie. La sorpresa!"

*waves happily toward the door*

"Arrivederci, Valentina!"

transcript.speaker: Valentina
transcript.raw:
~Valentina waves warmly from behind the counter.~

"Il Duomo! Dall'eleganza della moda all'eleganza gotica. Arrivederci — e buona fortuna con la sorpresa!"

[ENGLISH: The Duomo! From the elegance of fashion to Gothic elegance. Goodbye — and good luck with the surprise!]

[DEBRIEF]
{
  "learned": ["sto solo guardando — I'm just looking", "è bellissimo — it's beautiful", "troppo caro — too expensive", "posso provarlo — can I try it on", "il camerino — the fitting room", "lo prendo — I'll take it", "rosso — red", "taglia piccola — small size", "grazie mille — thank you very much", "arrivederci — goodbye"],
  "retry": ["quanto costa — how much does it cost (you said it well but hesitated)", "molto grazie — should be grazie mille (you caught it yourself — bravo!)", "perfecto — should be perfetto (you caught this one too!)"],
  "valentina_says": "Chad, you were an absolute delight — your enthusiasm was genuine, your Italian was braver than you give yourself credit for, and buying those gloves as a surprise for Charlie? That's the most romantic thing I've seen all week on Via della Spiga.",
  "transitionTo": "duomo"
}
[/DEBRIEF]
transcript.spoken:
~Valentina waves warmly from behind the counter.~

"Il Duomo! Dall'eleganza della moda all'eleganza gotica. Arrivederci — e buona fortuna con la sorpresa!"

learned: sto solo guardando — I'm just looking
posso provarlo — can I try it on
grazie mille — thank you very much
retry: quanto costa — how much does it cost (you said it well but hesitated)
molto grazie — should be grazie mille (you caught it yourself — bravo!)
perfecto — should be perfetto (you caught this one too!)
debrief.valentina_says: Chad, you were an absolute delight — your enthusiasm was genuine, your Italian was braver than you give yourself credit for, and buying those gloves as a surprise for Charlie? That's the most romantic thing I've seen all week on Via della Spiga.
